  • ROS ALTMANN

    Ros is a leading authority on later life issues, including pensions,
    social care and retirement policy. Numerous major awards have recognised
    her work to demystify finance and make pensions work better for people.
    She was the UK Pensions Minister from 2015 – 16 and is a member
    of the House of Lords where she sits as Baroness Altmann of Tottenham.

    • Ros Altmann

    VISIT ROS'S BLOG FOR HER VERY LATEST COMMENTS

    CLICK THIS LINK

    WELCOME

    Welcome to Ros Altmann's website

    Welcome to Ros Altmann's website. An award winning expert on all aspects of pensions and later life policy, from investment strategy to company pensions, state pension policy, retirement and social care. She is also famous as a champion for the rights of older people, having spearheaded a multi-year campaign to force Government to restore lost pensions to 140,000 victims of the company pensions scandal.

     READ HER PROFESSIONAL PROFILE 

    Follow Ros On Twitter

    READ MORE

    SOCIAL CARE - CRISIS AND REFORM
    Ros has been the recipient of a number of prestigious media awards for her pensions work

    READ MORE 

    PENSIONS VICTORY
    Ros forced the Government to pay 140,000 people whose pensions were confiscated by law, despite Government assurances of safety. Read the history of appeals to Parliamentary Ombudsman, High Court and Court of Appeal which the Government lost each time.

    READ MORE 

    PENSION FUND ASSET ALLOCATION AND INSTITUTIONAL INVESTMENT
    Ros' views on pension fund investment approaches, Liability Driven Investing, use of alternative assets and trends in asset allocation

    READ MORE

    INVESTMENT TRUSTS

    Ros highlights the important potential for UK investment companies to channel pension and retail long term investment to boost UK growth.

    READ MORE

    PENSIONS POLICY AND REFORM - RETIREMENT, SAVINGS, ANNUITIES
    Ros works on many policy areas. Click on the headings below to view any of the areas you are interested in.
    Pensions Policy and Reform – Retirement, Savings and Annuities
    Pension reform - State and private pension policy
    Women's pensions
    Auto enrolment
    Occupational pensions crisis - Defined Benefit Schemes
    Public Sector pension reform
    Annuities reform
    Savings Policy
    Pensions Administration
     
    MONETARY AND FISCAL POLICY
    Ros comments on Economic and Monetary Policy - particularly the impact of QE, interest rates, inflation and fiscal policy
